prose:
Method–provenance block
Property: Atom-condensed Fukui functions f⁺ (nucleophilic attack), f⁻ (electrophilic attack), f⁰, and the dual descriptor for formaldehyde; identification of the most electrophilic and most nucleophilic atomic sites.  
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP, tier `standard`, density-fitting (RIJK) on (PySCF default).  
Geometry provenance: single-point calculations on the user-supplied Cartesian geometry `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz`; no re-optimization at DFT (DFT//input-xyz).  
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation).  
Electronic state: neutral singlet (charge 0, multiplicity 1) for the N-electron reference; cation (+1, doublet) and anion (−1, doublet) states used for the N±1 calculations.  
Software: chemkit `fukui-reactivity` skill (PySCF backend).  
Convergence & caveats: SCF cycles for all three charge states converged; integrity check status *ok* (integrity.trustworthy = true).  
Warnings returned by the engine (VERBATIM):  
• "sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)"  
• "sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)"  
• "common header present"

Headline result
The most electrophilic site is the carbonyl carbon (atom 2, C) with f⁺ = 0.7083913, and the most nucleophilic site is the carbonyl oxygen (atom 1, O) with f⁻ = 0.46742464.  The headline f⁺ value is reported below.

Reproducibility
